title: Choose the binlog format for replication safety
description: ROW-based binary logging replicates exact changes and avoids the nondeterminism that breaks STATEMENT replication.
body: Use when configuring replication or chasing data drift between primary and replica. Do not run STATEMENT binlog format with nondeterministic statements (NOW(), UUID(), LIMIT without ORDER BY, triggers); the replica re-executes the SQL and can diverge. Actions: set binlog_format=ROW for safety, reserve MIXED only when binlog volume is a proven problem, and verify with mysqlbinlog that row images are written. Validation signals include checksums matching between primary and replica and Seconds_Behind_Master settling at zero. Risk boundary: switching binlog format on a live primary affects every downstream replica and any CDC consumer, so coordinate the change and roll forward, never silently.
